# Jaani Sepikoda — Portfolio (Vue 3 + Vuetify)

A blacksmith portfolio site for "Jaani Sepikoda" — a traditional Estonian smithy. Dark, minimalistic aesthetic reflecting the craft of forging steel.

## Stack

- **Framework**: Vue 3 with Composition API (`<script setup>`)
- **UI Library**: Vuetify 4 (Material Design icons via `md:` prefix)
- **Build**: Vite 7 with `vite-plugin-vuetify` (auto-import)
- **Routing**: Vue Router 4, HTML5 history mode, multilingual (`/:lang/...`)
- **Production URL**: `https://www.sepised.com`
- **Data Source**: Headless WordPress via WPGraphQL (`https://tagatuba.sepised.com/graphql`)
- **i18n**: Manual — JSON files in `src/i18n/` (et, en, ru), reactive `t` prop passed from `App.vue`
- **Path alias**: `@/` → `src/`

## Architecture

```
App.vue          — Root layout, data fetching, language state, nav
├── Home.vue     — Hero landing page (pages/)
├── Portfolio.vue — Filterable gallery grid (pages/)
├── Contact.vue  — Business card layout (pages/)
├── Product.vue  — Detail view with lightbox (components/)
├── Frontend.vue — Legacy category grid (components/, not routed)
└── Category.vue — Legacy category detail (components/, not routed)
```

- `App.vue` owns all shared state: `nodes`, `categories`, `lang`, `t`, `api`, `loading`
- All data is auto-fetched at startup by paginating through all GraphQL pages (100 per request)
- `loading` flag is `true` until all pages are fetched
- Each node has `slug` (from WordPress) used in URLs — never use `title` in routes
- Each node has a `categories` array (multi-category) — one image can belong to multiple categories
- Client-side pagination: Portfolio shows 30 items at a time with "Show More" button and count display
- Routes: `/` → redirect `/et`, `/:lang`, `/:lang/portfolio`, `/:lang/contact`, `/:lang/portfolio/:category/:product`

## Design System — "Forge & Steel"

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| `--color-charcoal` | `#121212` | Background |
| `--color-steel` | `#2c2c2e` | Cards, secondary surfaces |
| `--color-silver` | `#e0e0e0` | Body text |
| `--color-ember` | `#ff6d00` | Accent, active states, hover |
| `--color-ember-glow` | `rgba(255,109,0,0.4)` | Glow/shadow effects |
| Gold | `#FDDE7C` | Brand title only |

- **Brand font**: `goudy` (custom, loaded via `@font-face`)
- **Title font**: `arkhip` (custom TTF)
- **Body font**: `Roboto` / `Inter`
- **Background**: Full-viewport `tuli2.jpg` with `background-attachment: fixed`
- **Theme**: Vuetify dark theme; transparent header/footer over background image

## Code Conventions

### Vue Components
- Use **Composition API** with `<script setup>` syntax
- Use `ref`, `computed`, `watch`, `onMounted` from Vue
- Define props with `defineProps()` and emits with `defineEmits()`
- All user-facing strings go through the `t` translation object — never hardcode text in templates
- Props flow down from `App.vue`: `lang`, `t`, `categories`, `nodes`, `api`
- Emit events upward (e.g., `emit('load-more')`)

### i18n
- Static UI text: `src/i18n/{et,en,ru}.json` → accessed as `t.section.key`
- Dynamic content (from WPGraphQL): language-specific fields on nodes (`node.en`, `node.ru`, `node.et`)
- When adding new UI text, add the key to **all three** locale files

### Styling
- Global styles in `src/assets/css/style.css` using CSS custom properties
- Component `<style scoped>` for component-specific styles
- Use Vuetify utility classes (`bg-steel`, `text-caption`, `d-flex`, etc.)
- CSS class naming: `kebab-case` (`robust-border`, `minimal-button`, `smithy-title`)
- Aesthetic: robust, minimalistic, high-contrast — no rounded corners (sharp edges)

### Vuetify
- Icon prefix: `md:` (Material Design Icons via iconfont)
- Dark theme is the only theme
- Use Vuetify grid (`v-row`, `v-col`) for responsive layouts

## Build & Dev

```sh
npm run dev      # Vite dev server
npm run build    # Production build → dist/
npm run preview  # Preview production build
```

## Key Patterns

- **Navigation**: Desktop menu bar with category submenu (`v-menu open-on-hover`); mobile drawer (`v-navigation-drawer`)
- **Category filtering**: Chips with `active-chip` class in Portfolio; driven by `selectedCategory` and route query param. Nodes have `categories` array — filter with `.includes()`
- **Search**: Client-side across `title`, `en`, `ru` fields — searches all loaded data (full database)
- **Product navigation**: Prev/Next scoped to the current category (from route param `/:category`), not global
- **Lightbox**: `v-dialog fullscreen` with contained image and inline nav
- **Image sizes**: GraphQL returns `mediumUrl`, `largeUrl`, `hdUrl`, `sourceUrl` — use appropriate size per context
- **Responsive breakpoint**: Desktop/mobile menu toggle at 1440px via CSS media queries

## SEO & Sitemap

- Sitemap is generated by a WordPress plugin (`wordpress/sepised-sitemap.php`) on `tagatuba.sepised.com`
- All URLs use slug-based permanent links: `/:lang/portfolio/:category-slug/:product-slug`
- `index.html` has default Open Graph meta tags (title, description, locale)
- HTML5 history mode requires server catch-all: all paths → `index.html`

## Do NOT

- Switch to Options API — keep Composition API (`<script setup>`) consistent
- Add new dependencies without explicit approval
- Hardcode any user-facing strings — always use `t` translations
- Use `mdi-` icon prefix — use `md:` (Material Design iconfont)
- Change the background image strategy or the transparent header/footer pattern
